feature_name: Dual-mode operation: Root Complex and End Point
description: The controller operates in both Root Complex (RC) and End Point (EP) modes for system versatility. Mode selection is latched at boot via PCIESSMODE[1:0] and can be overridden by software. RC mode enables downstream configuration, memory windowing, and interrupt reception. EP mode exposes BARs for host mapping and supports legacy or native endpoint behavior. Initialization flows for each role are explicitly defined.
evidence: Section 1.3 Features; Section 1.4.1 PCIe Core; Section 2.11.1/2.11.2 Initialization Sequences
dependencies: Power domain ON, SerDes PLL locked, CMD_STATUS[LTSSM_EN]=1, DEVSTAT.PCIESSMODE
Remarks: Choose role early; some capability fields must be consistent with mode before link-up.

feature_name: x1/x2 link width with dynamic width conversion and automatic lane reversal
description: The PCIe link supports up to two lanes and can negotiate width during training. Dynamic-width conversion enables reliable operation with partial lanes. Automatic lane reversal eases PCB routing by allowing Rx/Tx lane order inversion. Polarity inversion on receive further mitigates routing constraints. Width and lane behaviors are reflected in LINK_CAP and Debug/LTSSM states.
evidence: Section 1.3 Features; Section 3.7.5 LINK_CAP; Appendix A LTSSM
dependencies: SerDes lane enable (PL_GEN2[LN_EN]); PL_LINK_CTRL[LNK_MODE]
Remarks: Validate negotiated width in LINK_STAT_CTRL before enabling high throughput DMA.

feature_name: Gen1/Gen2 link speed (2.5/5.0 GT/s) with directed speed change
description: The controller supports 2.5 GT/s and 5.0 GT/s per lane, with 8b/10b encoding. Speed can be software-directed to Gen2 after Gen1 link-up using PL_GEN2[DIR_SPD]. De-emphasis and TX swing settings are provided to meet Gen2 signal integrity. Speed status is reflected in LINK_STAT_CTRL[LINK_SPEED]. Ensure common-clock settings when using SSC.
dependencies: SerDes PLL locked at 2.5 GHz; PL_GEN2[DIR_SPD], LINK_CTRL2[TGT_SPEED], COMMON_CLK_CFG
Remarks: Start at Gen1 for robustness, then step to Gen2 under software control when link margin is verified.

feature_name: Address translation: 32 outbound regions and 4 inbound regions
description: Outbound translator maps 32 equal-sized windows (1/2/4/8 MB) from local bus to PCIe addresses. Inbound translator offers four regions keyed by BAR matches to map PCIe addresses into SoC memory. 64-bit addressing is supported via high offset registers. BAR0 is reserved to application/config space and is not remappable inbound. Special BAR1 shortcut allows start address inferencing.
dependencies: CMD_STATUS[OB_XLT_EN/IB_XLT_EN]=1; Proper BAR configuration; Window alignment rules
Remarks: Avoid transactions that cross translation-window boundaries; size and alignment must be enforced by driver.

feature_name: Configurable BARs with mask registers (DBI_CS2)
description: BAR mask registers allow software to predefine size/type/prefetchability prior to host enumeration. DBI_CS2 gate enables temporary access to BAR mask overlays for both EP and RC modes. BAR0 inbound is fixed to application/config registers; only bits [31:20] effective. Readback must be verified as BAR read returns masked result.
evidence: Section 2.7.3.1 BAR Mask Registers; CMD_STATUS[DBI_CS2]; BARx/BARx_MASK (3.3, 3.4)
dependencies: Enable CMD_STATUS[DBI_CS2]; write BAR_MASK then clear DBI_CS2; respect BAR0 exception
Remarks: Use test-pattern write to confirm mask effect; restore DBI_CS2 to 0 before link enumeration.

feature_name: Endian conversion support (little/big with byte/word swap modes)
description: While the PCIe core is little-endian on pins, the subsystem supports system big-endian through selectable swap granularity. ENDIAN[ENDIAN_MODE] controls 1/2/4/8-byte swizzle for outbound and inbound paths. Tables detail byte-lane mapping for both directions. This enables seamless integration with big-endian CPUs.
dependencies: System endianness setting; driver program ENDIAN before data traffic
Remarks: Audit DMA descriptors and payload formats when changing swizzle modes to avoid data corruption.

feature_name: MSI and legacy interrupt support (EP generation, RC reception)
description: EP can generate either MSI or legacy INTx messages; RC can receive both and service across multiple endpoints. MSI generation uses a programmed address/data pair and targets MSI_IRQ in RC or EP peer BAR0. Legacy generation is controlled via EP_IRQ_SET/CLR with status in EP_IRQ_STATUS. RC provides 14 mapped interrupt events aggregating MSI vectors and error/PM events.
dependencies: Configure MSI_CAP; program MSI_LOW32/UP32 and MSI_DATA; INTX_DIS per need; IRQ_EOI handling
Remarks: Ensure ordering: posted writes for data completion may be reordered ahead of MSI; use barriers or reads if strict ordering is required.

feature_name: Advanced Error Reporting (AER) with logging and masks
description: AER exposes detailed status, mask, and severity registers for both correctable and uncorrectable errors. First Error Pointer and header log capture assist root-cause analysis. Root complex also provides Root Error Command/Status and Error Source ID for system-level handling. Software can tune which errors generate messages and their fatality classification.
dependencies: Enable reporting in DEV_STAT_CTRL and ROOT_CTRL_CAP; unmask desired errors; service IRQs
Remarks: Clear sticky W1C bits carefully; read header logs before clearing to preserve forensic data.

feature_name: Baseline PCIe/PCI-compatible error reporting
description: The module also supports baseline error signaling compatible with PCI-style flows. DEV_STAT_CTRL enables reporting of correctable, non-fatal, fatal, and UR errors. STATUS_COMMAND holds command/status bits like SERR_EN, PAR_ERR_RESP, and interrupt disable for INTx. This ensures compatibility with standard OS drivers on RC side.
dependencies: Set DEV_STAT_CTRL[*_RP] and SERR_EN as required
Remarks: Coordinate AER and baseline enables to avoid duplicate handling or masked conditions.

feature_name: Power management: device D-states and ASPM L0s/L1
description: Supports D0/D1/D2/D3hot/cold device states with PME messaging as configured in PMCAP/PM_CTL_STAT. Link-level ASPM provides L0s and L1 autonomous entry with configurable exit latencies. Software can also drive link power transitions, and common-clock aspects are configurable. L2 is not supported; L3 is power-off state.
dependencies: Enable ASPM via DEV_STAT_CTRL and LINK_STAT_CTRL; set PM capabilities; common clock
Remarks: Tune latencies vs. wake requirements; ensure remote partner supports selected ASPM states.

feature_name: SerDes PLL configuration and status (KeyStone I)
description: An on-macro PLL multiplies the differential refclock to 2.5 GHz for both Gen1 and Gen2. Software enables ENPLL, selects MPY, and verifies LOCK via PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL/ST S. Reference clock quality and loop bandwidth settings are provided. Status flags cover Rx/Tx test fail, LOS detect, and sync.
dependencies: Stable refclock; unlock kicker before chip-level writes; delay for regulator settle; LOCK=1
Remarks: Use device-specific kicker mechanism before accessing chip-level SerDes regs; keep output de-emphasis consistent with board channel.

feature_name: PIPE and PHY loopback modes for test
description: PIPE loopback supports master/slave roles for symbol-level verification between devices. PHY loopback internally routes TX to RX enabling link testing without a partner, configured via SERDES_CFG and forced LTSSM state. PL_LINK_CTRL[LPBK_EN] provides link-layer loopback control. Debug registers and LTSSM state encoding assist validation.
dependencies: DISABLE traffic before entering; configure SERDES loopback; force PL_FORCE_LINK state
Remarks: Set loopback prior to any traffic to avoid sequence number mismatches; ensure BAR/limit ranges don’t absorb test transactions.

feature_name: Initialization sequences for RC and EP
description: Documented bring-up steps cover power/clock enable, role selection, SerDes PLL lock, LTSSM enable, and configuration register programming. RC flow includes enumeration of downstream devices and memory window programming. EP flow includes APP_RETRY_EN to temporarily NAK accesses during slow init. Debug registers indicate link-up (LTSSM L0).
dependencies: PSC power domain ON; PLL LOCK; CMD_STATUS and PL_LINK_CTRL sequencing
Remarks: Automate state polling with timeouts; honor 100 ms minimum after resets before issuing PCIe transactions.

feature_name: Transaction constraints: mastering, alignment, burst, byte strobes
description: Transactions require BUS_MASTER enable in STATUS_COMMAND before issuing outbound requests. Internal bus max transaction size is 128 bytes, with 120-byte max when start is sub-8B aligned. Only incremental bursts are supported; fixed/wrap cause undefined behavior. Byte enables must be contiguous with no holes within a write.
dependencies: Set BUS_MS; enforce 128B window/alignment in DMA; validate BE patterns
Remarks: Enforce alignment in driver and DMA descriptors to prevent bus lockups and data truncation.

feature_name: DMA usage: external EDMA for outbound; master port for inbound
description: The PCIe module has no internal DMA engine. In RC mode, an SoC DMA can move data via the PCIe slave port while inbound TLPs target the master port. Bandwidth targets are ~85% of 2.0/4.0 Gbps at Gen1/Gen2 per lane. Example EDMA configurations for memory read/write are provided.
dependencies: Configure OB translation; ensure DMA throughput; bus arbitration capacity
Remarks: Throttle payload sizes to device MAX_PAYLD/MRR settings to optimize completion turnaround and avoid splits.

feature_name: Application and configuration address space mapping (Address Space 0)
description: A fixed 16 KB Address Space 0 provides access to application registers, local config space, remote config space, and a 4 KB IO proxy window (RC only). These spaces are non-burst and require 32-bit accesses. RC can also reach EP application registers through config space upper region.
dependencies: Correct base at base_location; 32-bit access width; RC-only IO proxy via IOBASE
Remarks: Guard remote config/IO windows; avoid access when link is down to prevent bus stalls.

feature_name: TLP attribute controls: Relaxed Ordering and No Snoop
description: Global control of TLP attributes is provided via TLPCFG. RELAXED enables relaxed ordering on all outgoing TLPs, improving performance on some topologies. NO_SNOOP marks traffic as non-cache coherent. These attributes also appear in DEV_STAT_CTRL and should be coordinated with system coherency policies.
dependencies: Program TLPCFG before traffic; coordinate with RC memory attributes
Remarks: Disable relaxed ordering where software requires strict completion ordering for correctness.

feature_name: Timers and ACK/FTS tuning
description: ACK frequency and FTS counts can be tuned to optimize latency and stability. ACK_FREQ allows sending an ACK DLLP per N TLPs; NFTS and COMM_NFTS tune training sequences for L0s exits. SYM_NUM provides modifiers for Ack/Nak latency and replay timers. These knobs help adapt to different link partners and channel conditions.
dependencies: Link trained; COMMON_CLK considerations; stable LTSSM
Remarks: Start with defaults then incrementally adjust; monitor retry and replay counters to avoid regressions.

feature_name: Reset mechanisms: POR, system reset, and software hot reset
description: Supports cold/warm reset per PCIe spec and software-initiated downstream hot reset. RSTCMD[INIT_RST] asserts a hot reset toward downstream ports in RC. Soft reset preserves sticky MMR and PM logic. Power domain cycling resets the module but requires traffic quiescence and reinitialization.
dependencies: Ensure FLUSH_N=0; wait >=100 ms post-reset; PSC sequencing
Remarks: Quiesce inbound/outbound traffic on both link ends before power cycling to avoid hangs requiring POR.

feature_name: Interrupt architecture: 14 mapped events
description: Interrupt events aggregate legacy INTx, MSI groups (0–31 mapped 4-per-event), error classes, and PM/reset events. Status, enable-set, and enable-clr registers exist per group with EOI vector-based completion. This mapping standardizes host interrupt routing and simplifies ISR demux.
dependencies: Route events to CPU interrupt controller; clear status then write EOI with correct event number
Remarks: Document MSI vector to event mapping in driver to avoid mis-clearing shared event lines.

feature_name: Debug visibility: LTSSM state and PIPE signals
description: DEBUG0/DEBUG1 expose LTSSM state, training sequence detection, skip events, and PIPE control/status. Appendix A provides an explicit map from encoded LTSSM values to states (e.g., 0x11=L0). These registers aid in bring-up, compliance, and failure triage. Link force and compliance assists are available via PL_FORCE_LINK and LINK_CTRL2.
dependencies: Clock active; LTSSM enabled; read-only sampling cadence
Remarks: Poll at moderate rate to avoid bus overhead; snapshot before and after retrain or compliance entry.

feature_name: Message and vendor filter/allow controls
description: Filter mask registers allow software to drop or pass certain TLPs (CFG/IO/MSG) and to relax strict completion checks during debug. Controls exist to treat out-of-BAR/poisoned/function-mismatch requests as UR or supported. Additional masks govern vendor message drop/UR behavior and DLLP aborts.
dependencies: Program masks before link-up for policy; ensure consistency with AER
Remarks: Use cautiously in production; liberal masks may hide protocol issues and reduce robustness.

feature_name: Standards compliance and capabilities declaration
description: The IP targets PCI Express Base Spec 2.0, with Synopsys DWC dual core and TI SerDes versions noted. Capabilities are advertised via PCIE_CAP (device/port type, version) and LINK_CAP (max width/speeds, ASPM). Proper declaration ensures interoperability with host software and link partners.
dependencies: Correct fields set prior to enumeration; role-consistent settings
Remarks: Validate with PCI-SIG compliance tests; keep capability bits aligned with actual board design (lanes, refclk).

feature_name: Application register block and general-purpose registers
description: A set of application registers (e.g., CMD_STATUS, TLPCFG, PRIORITY, GPRx) configure core behavior and report activity. Activity and priority registers expose buffer status and transaction QoS knobs. GPR0–GPR3 provide scratch fields for firmware coordination. All are 32-bit access aligned.
evidence: Section 3.1 Register Summary; ACT_STATUS (3.1.10); PRIORITY (3.1.14); GPRx (3.1.20–3.1.23)
dependencies: Access via Address Space 0; 32-bit single accesses only
Remarks: Avoid burst or non-32-bit widths in this region; treat GPRs as volatile shared state between cores.

feature_name: IO proxy window for RC (4 KB)
description: RC exposes a 4 KB memory window that translates internal accesses into PCIe IO transactions. IOBASE holds the upper bits provided into IO TLP addressing. Inbound IO is not supported; EP IO windows are disallowed. This permits legacy device IO cycles from RC.
dependencies: RC mode; CFG_SETUP programmed for Type 0/1 config when needed
Remarks: Restrict to DWORD-aligned 32-bit accesses; do not use bursts; ensure downstream supports IO space.

feature_name: Priority and privilege controls for inbound master traffic
description: PRIORITY allows setting master transaction privilege and priority (0 highest to 7 lowest). PRIV and PRIVID fields control supervisor vs user access semantics on inbound requests. This helps integrate with secure interconnect and QoS policies on the SoC fabric.
dependencies: SoC interconnect recognizing PRIV/PRIVID; master port enabled
Remarks: Use conservative priorities initially; elevate only traffic that must preempt to avoid starving other masters.
